own_review.issue: These jobs keep `if: always()` while gaining `needs: check-format`. When check-format fails, all upstream test jobs are skipped but the *-result jobs still run (always() overrides skipped deps), then fail confusingly trying to download artifacts that were never uploaded. The added check-format entry in their needs does not prevent this.

Every job in the pipeline is now hard-gated and serialized behind a full-repo spotless run. A single formatting nit suppresses license check and all test feedback, and adds several minutes of latency to fully independent jobs. Design tradeoff (saves CI minutes) but should be a conscious choice; independent checks like license could stay parallel.

Redundant `check-format` in `needs` of unit-test, samples-test-job, integration-test-job, samples-test-result, integration-test-result, jacoco-result-merge — already transitively required via build-source/prepare jobs. Harmless clutter.

Fragile log parsing: the sed pattern 'Check if code needs formatting    Check if code aligns with code style   ...' targets the GitHub UI log prefix, which never appears in mvn.log produced by `mvn --log-file` (plain Maven output) — that sed is a no-op; `head -n -11` hardcodes Maven's error-footer length and is GNU-specific. Works today on ubuntu-latest but brittle.
Artifact upload runs unconditionally, storing mvn.log on every successful run too; should be gated on `steps.check.outcome == 'failure'`.
Step named 'Setup Maven' actually installs a JDK (setup-java), and the artifact is named 'checkstyle-result' though it contains spotless output — misleading names.
No Maven repository cache (`cache: maven` or actions/cache) unlike other jobs in this file — every run re-downloads all plugins/deps for the whole multi-module reactor.
Using actions/github-script@v7 with an explicit GITHUB_TOKEN just to call core.setFailed is overkill; `run: exit 1` suffices and the token is unused by the script.
New job uses checkout@v4/setup-java@v4/upload-artifact@v4 while the rest of the file and all other workflows use @v3 (and setup-java@v1) — mixed major versions in one workflow.

points.point: Use ./mvnw instead of bare mvn (repo convention, pinned Maven version)
points.note: Verified: mvnw exists at root and all dubbo-build invocations in this and other workflows use ./mvnw. Slight overstatement ('every other job') — bare mvn appears in 4 jacoco-helper spots in this same file — and ubuntu-latest does pre-install Maven, but the reproducibility/convention point is real.
Verified: 67/67 checkout uses are @v3, setup-java is @v3/@v1. Minor but real consistency issue.

points.point: upload-artifact@v4 should be @v3, all 34 occurrences are @v3
points.note: Count verified exactly (34 @v3), but this is the same finding as the previous point split in two; no extra credit.

points.point: sed-based error parsing is fragile; head -n -11 is GNU-only
points.note: Verified. Diagnosis slightly off (the prefix sed never matches mvn.log at all, since --log-file output lacks UI prefixes), but the fragility verdict and GNU-extension fact are correct.

points.point: Artifact upload runs unconditionally; gate on failure
Summary generation correct; consider linking the artifact
Spotless plugin confirmed configured, Java 21 activates the profile
points.note: Verification statement, not an issue; accurate (jdk [11,) profile, v2.42.0).

missed: if: always() result jobs run and fail on missing artifacts when check-format fails
pipeline-wide hard gate / serialization of independent jobs (license, error-code-inspecting, native-image-inspecting)
redundant check-format entries in needs of downstream jobs
exceeds: ./mvnw vs bare mvn convention point — real, verified, not in human reference and not in my own review

points.point: Action version inconsistency @v4 vs repo-wide @v3, with counts
points.note: Verified: 5 workflow files, checkout 100% @v3 (67), upload-artifact 100% @v3 (34). Accurate and well-evidenced.

points.point: Missing name: field on the new check-format job
points.note: Fabricated: the diff adds `name: Check if code needs formatting` to the job. The supporting claim is also false — several existing jobs (samples-test-prepare, integration-test-prepare, jacoco-result-merge) have no name, and license/build-source only gain names in this very PR. Absence-fabrication.

points.point: Fragile sed parsing hardcoding job/step names; head -n -11 assumption
points.note: Verified fragile; same caveat as v4 that the sed actually never matches mvn.log content, but the verdict is right.

points.point: Redundant needs: check-format on jobs with transitive dependency via build-source
points.note: Verified against the dependency graph; matches my finding.

points.point: check-format gates ALL jobs, serializing independent checks (license, error-code-inspecting, native-image-inspecting)
points.note: Verified; the most substantive design point either candidate made, matches my finding.

missed: if: always() result jobs run and fail on missing artifacts when check-format fails
artifact upload runs unconditionally (v4 and my review both caught it)

qualitative: The human reference for this CI-only PR is a single conversational reply with no substantive findings, so grading rests almost entirely on independent verification. Both candidates correctly verified the spotless setup and flagged the fragile sed/head log parsing and the @v4-vs-@v3 action inconsistency with accurate occurrence counts. v4's distinctive contribution is the verified ./mvnw-vs-mvn convention point (a genuine exceed), plus the unconditional artifact upload; v5's distinctive contributions are the redundant transitive needs and the pipeline-wide serialization design concern, which is the strongest single point either made. v5 is dragged back to parity by a flat absence-fabrication — claiming the new job lacks a name: field when the diff plainly adds one — while v4 padded with a duplicate point and two non-findings. Both missed the subtlest real bug: the if: always() result jobs will still run and choke on missing artifacts whenever check-format fails.
